queries: E2E pipeline Components of a Data Pipeline authoritative recent sources
action.query: E2E pipeline Components of a Data Pipeline authoritative recent sources

annotations.title: Data Pipelines Explained: Architecture, Types, Benefits, and How to Build One
An Overview of Data Pipeline Architecture
What is a Data Pipeline? | Teradata
What is a Data Pipeline? | Teradata

content.text:
A data pipeline is a series of processes that move data from one system to another, applying necessary transformations along the way. ([datamation.com](https://www.datamation.com/big-data/data-pipeline/?utm_source=openai)) The key components of a data pipeline include:

1. **Data Sources**: These are the origins of raw data, which can include databases, APIs, file systems, IoT devices, social media, and logs. They provide the input that fuels the data pipeline. ([dzone.com](https://dzone.com/articles/what-is-a-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

2. **Ingestion Layer**: Responsible for bringing data into the pipeline, this layer can handle batch ingestion (collecting and moving data at scheduled intervals) or streaming ingestion (collecting data continuously in real time). ([quartilex.com](https://www.quartilex.com/blog/understanding-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

3. **Processing Engines**: These systems or frameworks (e.g., Apache Spark, Flink, or Hadoop) are responsible for ingesting, processing, and managing the data. They perform operations like filtering, aggregation, and computation at scale. ([dzone.com](https://dzone.com/articles/what-is-a-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

4. **Storage Systems**: Provide the infrastructure for storing both raw and processed data, offering scalability, durability, and accessibility for storing vast amounts of data. Examples include data warehouses, data lakes, and specialized databases. ([dzone.com](https://dzone.com/articles/an-overview-of-key-components-of-a-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

5. **Transformation**: In this stage, raw data is cleaned, normalized, enriched, or reshaped to fit a desired format or structure, making it usable for analysis or storage. ([dzone.com](https://dzone.com/articles/what-is-a-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

6. **Workflow**: Defines the order and dependencies between pipeline processes, ensuring that tasks are executed in the correct sequence. ([teradata.com](https://www.teradata.com/insights/data-architecture/what-is-a-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

7. **Monitoring**: Ensures all pipeline stages are functioning correctly, tracking performance and catching errors early. ([teradata.com](https://www.teradata.com/insights/data-architecture/what-is-a-data-pipeline?utm_source=openai))

Understanding these components is crucial for building efficient and reliable data pipelines that can handle large volumes of data and provide valuable insights.
